topics.title: USMCA review raises trade uncertainty
topics.summary: The US declined to extend USMCA in its current form after a six-year review, starting a longer review period while Canada, Mexico and the US seek changes.
topics.discussionPrompt: Trade deals sound dry, but they can affect prices, jobs and local businesses. How much do you think regular people should follow negotiations like this?

topics.title: Toronto fans warned about World Cup heat
topics.summary: Fans heading to the Portugal-Croatia World Cup match in Toronto were warned that humidity could make temperatures feel near 40 C, with hydration stations and storm plans in place.
topics.discussionPrompt: Big sports events are exciting, but weather can change the whole experience. Would extreme heat make you skip an outdoor game?

topics.title: Ireland begins EU presidency with Ukraine focus
topics.summary: Ireland began its six-month EU Council presidency, with Volodymyr Zelenskyy urging Europe to keep promises on Ukraine's accession talks and support.
topics.discussionPrompt: Joining a big international club can take years. Should the EU move faster for countries under pressure, or stick carefully to the usual process?

topics.title: Heat dome threatens holiday weekend
topics.summary: Forecasters warned that a heat dome could bring dangerously high temperatures across the eastern United States and parts of Canada during the Independence Day weekend.
topics.discussionPrompt: Holiday weekends are supposed to be relaxing. How do you change your plans when the weather turns from uncomfortable to genuinely dangerous?

topics.title: Markets pause as oil prices fall
topics.summary: Global stocks were little changed as investors weighed Federal Reserve comments, while oil prices fell on signs of easing supply concerns tied to US-Iran talks.
topics.discussionPrompt: Markets often react to hints and expectations, not just facts. Do you find that fascinating, frustrating, or mostly irrelevant to everyday life?
